With FirstCash trading at US$181.89 and carrying a value score of 1 plus an analyst price target of US$201.40, the key question is whether the recent momentum still leaves upside on the table or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Price-to-Earnings of 24.2x: Is It Justified?

At a last close of $181.89, FirstCash Holdings is trading on a P/E of 24.2x, which screens as expensive against several benchmarks and raises questions about how much future profit growth is already reflected in the price.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price to earnings per share, so you are effectively seeing how many dollars investors are willing to pay for each dollar of earnings. For a company like FirstCash, with pawn operations and retail POS payment solutions, this ratio gives a quick feel for how the market is weighing its earnings track record and forecast profit growth.

On one side, FirstCash is described as having high quality earnings, with earnings forecast to grow 16.66% per year and revenue forecast to grow 9.8% per year. Earnings growth over the past 5 years is reported at 20.1% per year, and earnings growth over the past year of 27.6% sits above that 5 year average. That kind of profile can support a higher P/E if investors expect the trend in profitability to continue.

On the other side, several reference points suggest the current P/E could be demanding. First, compared to the estimated fair P/E of 15.9x, the current 24.2x is higher, which implies the market price is rich relative to what the SWS fair ratio model suggests the multiple could move toward. Second, against the US Consumer Finance industry average P/E of 8x, FirstCash trades at a much higher level, pointing to a premium that assumes stronger or more resilient earnings than the typical peer. The shares are also trading above the SWS DCF model estimate of future cash flow value of $83.26, which is another sign that the current price bakes in optimistic expectations.

Put simply, the market is assigning FirstCash a P/E multiple that is well above both the sector average and the estimated fair multiple. Anyone looking at the stock needs to decide whether the company’s growth profile and earnings quality are enough to support that premium or whether the valuation could eventually settle closer to the fair ratio level suggested by the model.

data.title: Ferrotec (TSE:6890) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative In Q3 2026
data.description:
Ferrotec (TSE:6890) has put up a busy Q3 2026, with revenue of ¥70,665 million, basic EPS of ¥81.94 and net income of ¥3,838 million setting the tone for its latest update. Over the past few quarters, the company has seen revenue move between ¥67,208 million and ¥74,047 million, while basic EPS has ranged from ¥57.11 to ¥92.33, giving investors a clear view of how the top and bottom lines have tracked into this result. With net margin at 4.7% over the last year compared to 5.6% a year earlier, the focus this quarter is on how the market interprets the balance between growth expectations and pressure on profitability.

See our full analysis for Ferrotec.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely followed narratives around Ferrotec, highlighting where the data supports the story and where it raises fresh questions.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

TTM earnings slide against growth hopes

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Ferrotec earned ¥13,226 million in net income and ¥282.44 in basic EPS, compared with ¥15,692 million and ¥334.11 one year earlier, while five year earnings have declined about 5.4% per year.
  • What stands out is how this history interacts with the bullish view that earnings can grow about 12.9% per year, as:
    • Bulls point to forecast revenue growth of roughly 8.8% per year and TTM revenue of ¥283,670 million, higher than the ¥274,390 million level a year earlier, as support for a growth story despite the earnings slide.
    • That said, the drop in TTM net income from ¥15,692 million to ¥13,226 million means the bullish case is leaning on forward expectations while the recent track record still shows pressure on profitability.

Investors who want to see how optimistic and cautious market views stack up against these numbers can check the full narrative for Ferrotec in our balanced breakdown of growth, profitability and valuation signals. 📊 Read the full Ferrotec Consensus Narrative.

Margins and cash coverage stay tight

  • Net margin over the last year sits at 4.7% compared with 5.6% a year earlier, and the roughly 2.34% dividend yield is flagged as not well covered by free cash flow.
  • Bears focus on this pressure by arguing that thinner margins and weaker cash coverage leave less room for error, because:
    • The slip in net margin alongside the TTM net income move from ¥15,692 million to ¥13,226 million suggests profitability has been under strain even as revenue has grown.
    • With dividends not clearly backed by free cash flow, critics see an extra layer of risk around how comfortably Ferrotec can support shareholder payouts while funding its operations.

Mixed valuation signals at ¥6,320

  • The shares trade at ¥6,320 with a trailing P/E of 22.4x, sitting below the 30.1x peer average and the 23.2x industry average, while the DCF fair value of about ¥2,670.19 is far below the current price.
  • Supporters point out that the lower P/E versus peers helps a bullish argument, but the gap to DCF fair value pulls the other way, since:
    • Relative valuation looks more supportive, with the 22.4x P/E discount to peers and the semiconductor industry aligning with the idea that investors are not paying a premium multiple for the forecast 12.9% earnings growth.
    • At the same time, the current price sitting well above the ¥2,670.19 DCF fair value highlights a numerical disconnect that more cautious investors may treat as a reason to question how much of the growth story is already reflected in the share price.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on Ferrotec's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

data.title: Is Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) Pricing Reflect Lofty Expectations After Las Vegas Sphere Buzz?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Sphere Entertainment's share price matches its underlying worth, you are not alone. This article is designed to help you frame that question clearly.
  • The stock last closed at US$115.70, with returns of 24.1% over 7 days, 17.8% over 30 days, 22.7% year to date and 140.0% over 1 year. These figures naturally raise questions about how much future upside or downside is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Sphere Entertainment's high profile Sphere venue in Las Vegas and broader interest in live entertainment experiences. This has kept the company on many investors' radars. This backdrop helps explain why some market participants are rethinking the balance between potential growth and the risks tied to a single flagship asset.
  • Our valuation model gives Sphere Entertainment a valuation score of 2 out of 6, which reflects the number of checks where the stock screens as undervalued. Next, we will walk through the different approaches behind that score and touch on a more holistic way to think about value at the end of the article.

Sphere Entertainment scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Sphere Entertainment Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Sphere Entertainment, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $272.47 million, so the valuation leans heavily on future expectations rather than current cash generation.

Analyst and extrapolated estimates suggest free cash flow moving into positive territory over the coming years, reaching $469.00 million by 2030. Intermediate projections include $151.80 million in 2026 and $408.00 million in 2029, with later years extrapolated by Simply Wall St rather than directly forecast by analysts.

When all projected cash flows are discounted back using this 2 stage model, the estimated intrinsic value comes out at about $201.05 per share. Compared to the recent share price of $115.70, this implies the stock screens as about 42.5% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Sphere Entertainment is undervalued by 42.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Sphere Entertainment.

Approach 2: Sphere Entertainment Price vs Earnings (P/E)

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of earnings, because it ties the share price directly to the underlying profit figure.

A higher or lower P/E can often be linked to what the market expects for future earnings growth and how risky those earnings appear. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk tends to line up with a lower, more cautious multiple.

Sphere Entertainment currently trades on a P/E of 122.94x, compared with an Entertainment industry average of 32.74x and a peer group average of 43.93x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 5.82x. This is the P/E it would expect given factors such as the company’s earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison, because it attempts to adjust for company specific growth, risk and profitability rather than assuming one size fits all.

When set against the Fair Ratio of 5.82x, the current P/E of 122.94x suggests Sphere Entertainment screens as expensive on this earnings based view.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: SMC Corporation Just Beat EPS By 19%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
data.description:
It's been a good week for SMC Corporation (TSE:6273) shareholders, because the company has just released its latest quarterly results, and the shares gained 9.5% to JP¥70,840. It looks like a credible result overall - although revenues of JP¥210b were in line with what the analysts predicted, SMC surprised by delivering a statutory profit of JP¥671 per share, a notable 19% above expectations. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analysts are expecting for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from SMC's 17 analysts is for revenues of JP¥895.4b in 2027. This reflects a notable 10% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to rise 5.6% to JP¥2,636. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥889.5b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥2,612 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

View our latest analysis for SMC

The consensus price target rose 8.4% to JP¥66,450despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of SMC's earnings by assigning a price premium. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. Currently, the most bullish analyst values SMC at JP¥100,000 per share, while the most bearish prices it at JP¥48,000. This is a fairly broad spread of estimates, suggesting that analysts are forecasting a wide range of possible outcomes for the business.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. The analysts are definitely expecting SMC's growth to accelerate, with the forecast 8.2% annualised growth to the end of 2027 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 5.7% per annum over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 5.1% per year. Factoring in the forecast acceleration in revenue, it's pretty clear that SMC is expected to grow much faster than its industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple SMC analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

Don't forget that there may still be risks. For instance, we've identified 1 warning sign for SMC that you should be aware of.

data.title: Capgemini (ENXTPA:CAP) Margin Slippage Challenges Bullish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Capgemini (ENXTPA:CAP) has reported its FY 2025 results with second half revenue of €11.4b and basic EPS of €5.20, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of €22.5b and EPS of €9.45 that investors will be weighing against recent margin trends. Over the past three reported halves, revenue has moved from €11.0b in H2 2024 to €11.1b in H1 2025 and €11.4b in H2 2025. Basic EPS has shifted from €4.93 to €4.26 and then €5.20, setting up a picture where modest revenue progression meets earnings that are being closely watched against a net profit margin of 7.1% versus 7.6% a year earlier.

See our full analysis for Capgemini.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely shared narratives around Capgemini, highlighting where the data backs the story and where it starts to push back.

See what the community is saying about Capgemini

Advertisement

7.1% net margin now sits below last year’s 7.6%

  • Over the last 12 months, Capgemini converted €22.5b of revenue into €1.6b of net income, for a 7.1% net profit margin compared with 7.6% a year earlier, while trailing EPS went from €9.82 to €9.45.
  • Consensus narrative describes a shift toward higher value cloud, data and AI work as a potential support for future margin resilience. However, the current 7.1% margin and slightly lower trailing EPS indicate that near-term pricing pressure and restructuring costs are still visible in the reported numbers.
    • Analysts are assuming margins eventually reach 8.2%, which would be a step up from the 7.1% reported over the last year.
    • The group’s focus on automation and higher margin digital services is intended to offset current margin pressure, but the latest figures show that effect is not yet reflected in the trailing margin.

TTM revenue at €22.5b, forecast growth of 4.7% a year

  • On a trailing basis, revenue is €22.5b and is described as having grown about 4.7% per year over the last 12 months, which sits below the referenced French market rate of 5.7% a year.
  • Bulls argue that stronger demand for cloud, data and AI work, together with higher growth in regions like Asia Pacific and Latin America, can support faster top line progress. The recent 4.7% revenue growth rate and guidance that acknowledges softer conditions in parts of Europe indicate that any acceleration still has to come through in the reported revenue trend.
    • The bullish view expects revenue to climb faster over time, but the latest period still shows relatively modest growth compared with the wider French market.
    • With manufacturing, automotive and some European markets described as soft, the current growth profile leaves less room for error if those weaker areas do not improve.
Have a look at how bullish investors are framing that potential, and where they think growth could surprise, in 🐂 Capgemini Bull Case.

P/E of 11x, well below DCF fair value estimate

  • At a share price of €104.85, Capgemini trades on a P/E of 11x, with analysis indicating this sits below a DCF fair value of about €212.03 and below an analyst price target of €169.67, while still being slightly higher than the cited peer P/E of 10.3x and lower than the wider European IT average of 18.5x.
  • Bears caution that slower revenue growth and modest margin compression could justify a lower multiple, and they point to recent margin softness and below market revenue growth as backing for that stance, even though the current 11x P/E is materially beneath both the DCF fair value indication and the €169.67 analyst target.
    • The small gap between Capgemini’s 11x P/E and the 10.3x peer average is consistent with concerns about margins and growth running a little behind some peers.
    • At the same time, the large difference between the current price and both the DCF fair value and analyst target shows why many investors still see room for upside if growth and margins track closer to expectations.
If you are weighing that more cautious angle, it is worth seeing how skeptics frame the risks in 🐻 Capgemini Bear Case.

data.title: Kajima Corporation Just Beat Analyst Forecasts, And Analysts Have Been Updating Their Predictions
data.description:
A week ago, Kajima Corporation (TSE:1812) came out with a strong set of quarterly numbers that could potentially lead to a re-rate of the stock. The company beat forecasts, with revenue of JP¥773b, some 2.8% above estimates, and statutory earnings per share (EPS) coming in at JP¥96.29, 31% ahead of expectations. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. We've gathered the most recent statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their earnings models, following these results.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Taking into account the latest results, Kajima's seven analysts currently expect revenues in 2027 to be JP¥3.03t, approximately in line with the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are expected to shrink 3.4% to JP¥360 in the same period. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥3.04t and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥363 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

See our latest analysis for Kajima

The consensus price target rose 5.0% to JP¥6,593despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of Kajima's earnings by assigning a price premium. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. The most optimistic Kajima analyst has a price target of JP¥9,900 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at JP¥3,300. As you can see the range of estimates is wide, with the lowest valuation coming in at less than half the most bullish estimate, suggesting there are some strongly diverging views on how analysts think this business will perform. As a result it might not be a great idea to make decisions based on the consensus price target, which is after all just an average of this wide range of estimates.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. We would highlight that Kajima's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 0.09% annualised growth rate until the end of 2027 being well below the historical 9.9% p.a. growth over the last five years. Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 3.5% annually. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it seems obvious that Kajima is also expected to grow slower than other industry participants.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

With that in mind, we wouldn't be too quick to come to a conclusion on Kajima. Long-term earnings power is much more important than next year's profits. We have forecasts for Kajima going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

data.title: Is AGNC Investment (AGNC) Attractively Priced After Strong One Year Share Price Performance?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether AGNC Investment's share price really reflects its underlying value, you are not alone. Many income focused investors keep a close eye on this mortgage REIT.
  • The stock last closed at US$11.36, with returns of 3.9% year to date and 26.0% over 1 year. Shorter term moves have been softer, with a 0.7% decline over 7 days and a 1.8% decline over 30 days.
  • Recent headlines around AGNC have focused on its role as a mortgage REIT in a changing interest rate backdrop, including commentary on how its portfolio positioning and leverage policy respond to shifts in bond markets. These themes help frame why the share price has seen both longer term gains and shorter term pullbacks as investors reassess risk and income prospects.
  • On our checks, AGNC scores a 5 out of 6 valuation rating. This suggests it screens as undervalued across most of our metrics. Next we will walk through the key valuation approaches before finishing with a broader framework that can help you judge its value more clearly.

AGNC Investment delivered 26.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Mortgage REITs industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: AGNC Investment Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to generate above the return that equity investors require, then capitalizes those surplus profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For AGNC Investment, the starting point is its book value of $9.41 per share and a stable earnings figure of $1.51 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 4 analysts. The average Return on Equity is 15.67%, compared with a cost of equity of $0.93 per share. That gap is the excess return, estimated at $0.59 per share.

The model also uses a stable book value of $9.66 per share, again drawn from weighted future Book Value estimates from 4 analysts. Together, these inputs are used to project the stream of future excess returns and convert them into an estimated intrinsic value of about $19.10 per share.

A big dividend yield for a few years doesn't mean much if it can't be sustained. However, Watami's earnings easily cover the dividend. This means that most of what the business earns is being used to help it grow.

The company's dividend history has been marked by instability, with at least one cut in the last 10 years. The last annual payment of ¥10.00 was flat on the annual payment from10 years ago. We're glad to see the dividend has risen, but with a limited rate of growth and fluctuations in the payments the total shareholder return may be limited.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

Given that the dividend has been cut in the past, we need to check if earnings are growing and if that might lead to stronger dividends in the future. It's encouraging to see that Watami has been growing its earnings per share at 68% a year over the past five years. Rapid earnings growth and a low payout ratio suggest this company has been effectively reinvesting in its business. Should that continue, this company could have a bright future.
